activities_projects.title: Critical Interpretation
steps: Read the poem carefully
Explain how human emotions can destroy the world
Write 5–6 sentences analysing the idea

activities_projects.title: Theme Exploration
steps: Identify the main theme
Explain how the poet uses fire and ice to present it
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands deeper meaning of the poem

activities_projects.title: Literary Device Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyse poetic devices in the poem
steps: Identify two literary devices
Explain their effect on the poem

activities_projects.title: Philosophical Meaning
activities_projects.objective: Understand philosophical ideas in the poem
steps: Explain the philosophical meaning of destruction
Write 5 sentences explaining how emotions influence human behaviour

activities_projects.title: Creative Expansion
steps: Imagine another way the world could end
Write a short 4-line poem about it

activities_projects.title: Contrast Study
steps: Explain how fire and ice contrast each other
Write 5 sentences describing the contrast

activities_projects.title: Moral Debate
activities_projects.objective: Discuss moral lessons from the poem
steps: Write arguments about which emotion is more destructive
Support your answer with examples

activities_projects.title: Short Essay
steps: Write a short essay of 6–7 sentences
Topic: Human emotions and destruction in Fire and Ice

activities_projects.title: Modern Relevance
steps: Think about conflicts in the modern world
Explain how they relate to fire and ice

activities_projects.title: Critical Reflection
steps: Write your opinion about the poem
Explain why the poem is meaningful in 5–6 sentences
